# tg_cluster_connector (Resource)

Manage a forwarding connector on a cluster (V2). Requires the cluster to have been upgraded to V2 connectors config — see `tg_cluster_connectors_v2_upgrade`.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "tg_cluster_connector" "tomcat" {
  cluster_fqdn = "hq.example.test"
  node         = "local"
  service      = "127.0.0.1:8080"
  port         = 8081
  protocol     = "tcp"
  description  = "tomcat forwarding connector"
  nic          = "eth0"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `cluster_fqdn` (String) FQDN of the cluster that owns this connector.
- `node` (String) Node identifier (`local` or a node UID).
- `port` (Number) Listening port on the gateway (1-65535).
- `protocol` (String) Protocol. One of: udp, tcp, tftp, ftp, rdp, vnc, ssh.
- `service` (String) Upstream service address (`host:port`).

### Optional

- `description` (String) Description.
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the connector is enabled.
- `nic` (String) NIC to listen on.
- `rate_limit` (Number) Max throughput in Mbps (0 for unlimited).

### Read-Only

- `connector_id` (String) Connector unique ID. Computed after create.
- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
